comments.comment: The TPM released to the environment is mainly a result of road dust. Road dust calculations are specific to the weather for the year and the equipment movement on the mine site. The weather in 2014 was generally drier than in 2013 (275 wet/frozen days in 2013 vs. 209 wet/frozen days in 2014). Equipment movement is dependant on a number of factors, such as production and project work. Because of all of these factors, the road dust totals were greater in 2014 as compared to 2013. Note that Voisey's Bay continued to mitigate road dust by the use of both environmentally friendly chemical suppressants and watering frequently.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le recyclage

comments.comment: Waste ethylene glycol comes from the glycol heating system that is used to heat the service buildings.  Every few years, there is a requirement to complete a changeout of the glycol in the loop which produce large volumes of glycol to be recycled.  This was the case in 2011.  In 2012, much smaller volumes of glycol were removed from the system and hence, much lower volumes of glycol were produced for recycling.  2012 values are in line with 2010 values.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Most of the on site releases of nitrous oxides are from power generation (point source).  The amount of power generated on site is dependant upon production levels at the mine/mill site.  In addition, a stack sampling program was completed in 2012 that saw a slight increase in the emission rate of NOx from diesel generator stacks.  This caused an overall increase in the amount of NOx released from stacks.  Note that NOx emissions are still within provincial limits.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Propylene released to air is a result of fuel tank emissions and emissions from gensets.  The amount of fuel used and the power generation (genset use) is dependant on production levels.

type.fr: Commentaire sur le statut de déclaration de la substance

comments.comment: Note that propylene was not manufactured on site and the total propylene released was less than 10 kg.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Hexachlorobenzene released is a result of incinerator stack emissions.  The quantity released depends on the amount of waste incinerated.  In 2012, there was more waste incinerated than in 2011.  This is likely a result of an increase in Capital construction, resulting in a full camp population for most of the year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: Most of the on site releases of carbon monoxide are from blasting activities (fugitive) and power generation (point).  The amount of blasting activities and power generation on site are dependant upon production levels at the mine/mill site.  In addition, a stack sampling program completed in 2012 saw a reduction in the emission rate of CO from diesel generator stacks.  This caused an overall reduction in point source CO emissions.

Lead released on site is mostly from concentrate/ore handling.  The amount of lead present in concentrate/ore varies from year to year based on production and the grade of ore being processed.

There was a reduction in the amount of lead released through direct discharge to water bodies in 2012.  This quantity is based on weekly samples collected from the final discharge point.  This reduction could be a result of many factors, including: varying amounts of lead in the incoming ore and hence varying amount of lead in the tailings (waste) stream, and higher lead recovery to concentrate.  This is expected to vary year to year.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The TPM released to the environment is mainly a result of road dust.  Road dust calculations are specific to the weather for the year and the equipment movement on the mine site.  The weather in 2012 was generally drier than in 2011 (260 wet/frozen days in 2012 vs. 292 wet/frozen days in 2011).  Equipment movement is dependant on a number of factors, such as production and project work.  In 2012, there was a significant increase in project work with the construction of a pebble crusher extension to the Mill complex.  Because of all of these factors, the road dust totals are higher in 2012 as compared to 2011.  Note that Voisey's Bay continued to mitigate road dust by the use of both environmentally friendly chemical suppressants and watering daily.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The PM10 released to the environment is mainly a result of road dust.  Road dust calculations are specific to the weather for the year and the equipment movement on the mine site.  The weather in 2012 was generally drier than in 2011 (260 wet/frozen days in 2012 vs. 292 wet/frozen days in 2011).  Equipment movement is dependant on a number of factors, such as production and project work.  In 2012, there was a significant increase in project work with the construction of a pebble crusher extension to the Mill complex.  Because of all of these factors, the road dust totals are higher in 2012 as compared to 2011.  Note that Voisey's Bay continued to mitigate road dust by the use of both environmentally friendly chemical suppressants and watering daily.

In addition, a stack sampling program was completed in 2012 which saw a slight increase in the emission rate for PM10 from diesel generator stacks.  This resulted in an overall increase in PM10 released from point sources.  Note that PM10 emissions from diesel stacks are still within the provincial limits.
